summary: This post serves as a companion reading list to Fowler's article on Language Workbenches, collecting resources on various tools and approaches in this emerging field. It covers Intentional Software's work, JetBrains' Meta Programming System, Microsoft's Software Factories initiative, and MDA-based approaches. The post also highlights community contributions including Java ports of examples and Lisp implementations. Fowler signals his ongoing interest in tracking developments in Language Oriented Programming.
key_points: Intentional Software was emerging from stealth mode with promising work on language workbenches
JetBrains' MPS represented a strong vision for Language Oriented Programming with active community support
Microsoft's Software Factories initiative generated significant discussion, including debates about MDA usage
Multiple approaches existed including OMG standards-based tools like Xactium
Community contributions were expanding the practical examples across different languages
money_quotes: For the last few years Intentional Software has been hiding under some deep rocks. They are beginning to peep out now.
Tracing through the Software Factory blogs will also give you some insights as to the debates over the use of MDA, since much of that discussion has been around the Microsoft work.
I intend to keep an eye on this field and expect to post further material on martinfowler.com.
key_insight: Language workbenches were an emerging field in 2005 with multiple competing approaches from Intentional Software, JetBrains, and Microsoft, each representing different visions for Language Oriented Programming.
